element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player begins armed; establishes baseline damage output and range combat
First enemy type; simple behavior (approach and attack); low threat individually
Escalation; more aggressive behavior and varied attack types
Increased damage; first significant weapon upgrade
Risk/reward melee option; close-quarters combat strategy
Level gating mechanic; navigation puzzle element
Environmental hazard; damage over time; requires navigation awareness
Timed hazard; requires pattern recognition and timing
Mid-game power spike; rapid-fire ranged weapon
Late-game; crowd control and boss damage; rare ammo
Toxic waste immunity; temporary protective buff
Chainsaw efficiency boost; encourages aggressive play
Episode climax; requires mastery of weapon loadout and positioning
Final episode; highest enemy density and difficulty spike

typical_relief_triggers: Finding health packs after intense combat
Reaching safe rooms before next enemy wave
Using invulnerability during heavy enemy spawns
typical_tension_triggers: Loud enemy death screams signal more incoming enemies
Opening doors into new chambers (unknown enemy placement)
Hearing monster groans and growls before visibility
Low ammo combined with large enemy groups
Proximity to toxic waste or crushing hazards
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Waves of combat interspersed with navigation/exploration phases. Early episodes favor exploration and puzzle-solving (keycard locks, hazard navigation). Later episodes compress combat and reduce safe corridors, escalating tension density. Boss fights create climactic peaks at episode conclusions.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Side passages and branching corridors often conceal weapon caches, armor, ammo, and secret areas. Player incentivized to explore dead-ends and off-path spaces for resource advantage.
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden walls (visually indistinguishable from solid walls) contain secret rooms with power-ups and weapons. Discoverable by collision testing or indicated by subtle visual asymmetries. Secrets reward thorough exploration.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Episode 1 ('Knee-Deep in the Dead'): Introduction to core mechanics with manageable enemy counts and spacing. Single weapon types dominate early encounters. Hazards (toxic waste, crushers) teach spatial awareness.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Episode 2 ('The Shores of Hell'): Enemy variety increases; encounters feature mixed demon/human groups. Weapon progression accelerates. Level complexity introduces multi-room ambush zones. Resource scarcity becomes noticeable.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Episode 3 ('Inferno'): Hell setting escalates enemy density and aggression. Boss fights require precise weapon selection and positioning. Ammo conservation becomes critical. Minimal safe rooms force sustained combat flow.
notable_spikes: Episode 3, Level 8 (Boss Spider Mastermind) - hardest encounter in base game
Sudden ambush rooms with 10+ enemies spawning simultaneously in later levels

notable_level_patterns.description: Central chamber with multiple branching corridors leading to keycarded areas. Player must backtrack through hub to access new zones. Encourages vertical navigation and spatial memory.
Corridor-heavy design forcing player through sequential combat rooms with limited backtracking. Escalating enemy density pushes forward momentum.
Levels utilizing platforms, bridges, and elevation changes. Combat occurs across multiple heights. Hazards (toxic waste) occupy lower tiers, forcing platforming awareness.
Final level of each episode culminates in a distinct arena separated from main level. Contains boss enemy requiring learned combat pattern recognition.
Non-critical hidden chambers accessible only through exploration or wall-collision testing. Contain weapon upgrades, ammo, and health surplus. Rewards curiosity without blocking progression.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All levels contain 2-5 secrets per level
